A population of Great Crested Newts is present within the pond, hedgerows and scrub within Werrington Triangle. Activities within this area should not injure or kill, or disturb the newts or their eggs. For more information please contact Jim Pearson Network Rail Environment Manager 07876195286.

Newt Fencing will be installed from 28th August 18, in order to trap and relocate a population of Great Crested Newts from Werrington Triangle. The fencing will be 60cm in height and will contain gates to be able to access the track from the triangle. The fencing needs to be in place for at least 30 days and will be removed when 5 newt free days has been achieved.

The works comprise four parallel directional drill bores, each to carry an array of three 180mm and two number 110mm OD ducts in a pentagonal arrangement below the existing east coast main line rail tracks near Station Road, Essendine, Lincolnshre. The works will be carried out on behalf of National Grid for the ultimate benefit of Network Rail. Each directional drill will be reamed to a maximum of 425 mm over a shot. length of approx 75m with an axial spacing of 6m and will cross normal to the tracks, that is at 90 degrees to the track to provide a minimum cover to the crown of the installed ducts of 6.0m and an associated estimated settlement of less than 5mm. The 180mm diameter ducts will carry 25kv High voltage electrical cables. The 120mm ducts will carry associated monitoring and managment cable.
